name: Hallas ibn Amr
title: Officer of Amir al-Mu'minin
shortDescription: A member of the Shurtat al-Khamis (special forces) of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who, along with his brother Nu'man, joined the Hussaini caravan and was martyred.
fullStory:
Hallas ibn Amr al-Rasibi was a member of the special forces and ceremonial guard officers (Shurtat al-Khamis) during the caliphate of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). He lived in Kufa and had a very deep, heartfelt devotion to the Prophet's (PBUH) family. His brilliant record of serving Imam Ali (AS) demonstrated his bravery and adherence to Wilayah.

When Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad took control of Kufa, Hallas and his brother Nu'man decided to leave the city to help Imam Hussain (AS). Despite the atmosphere of terror ruling Kufa, they left the city secretly at night.

They reached Karbala and took their place alongside Imam Hussain's (AS) loyal companions. The presence of veteran and loyal soldiers like Hallas was a source of encouragement for the other companions of the Imam.

On the day of Ashura, Hallas, who had years of combat experience alongside the Lion of God, Ali (AS), bravely drew his sword. In the collective attack of the Kufan army (the first attack), he was in the front lines defending the tents.

He fought with great skill and, after repelling consecutive enemy attacks, was martyred by multiple sword and spear strikes to seal his pact with Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) with his own blood alongside his son.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى حَلّاسِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو الرّاسِبِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Hallas ibn Amr al-Rasibi.
